title: Guard against catastrophic forgetting
description: Mix in general data, limit update scope, and use low learning rates so task adaptation does not erase pretrained capabilities.
body: Use when fine-tuning on a narrow task corpus. Avoid training many epochs at a high learning rate on task-only data — the model overfits the task and forgets general behavior. Actions: prefer low-rank adapters, keep the learning rate small, blend a slice of general/instruction data, and early-stop on a capability holdout. Validation signals include a stable capability-holdout score across training and a task gain achieved without the general metric dropping. Risk boundary: forgetting is often discovered only after deployment on adjacent tasks; if the capability holdout falls during training, halt and treat it as a blocking signal rather than training longer.
